purpose: Scenario fixtures for stronger-agent judgment. These are not trigger-routing fixtures.

replay_contract.purpose: Convert a source-evolution claim into evidence that one or more judgment cases changed problem reframing, upstream redefinition, alternative-path comparison, strongest counterargument keep/narrow/park call, evidence weighting, boundary call, default override, or scaffold treatment.
evidence_boundary: replay can support intelligence-ceiling or half-life improvement for the tested claim
replay does not prove broad product-goal improvement without applied research or development problem evidence
passing source verifiers remains source-integrity evidence, not replay evidence

cases.scenario: The user asks for implementation, but the upstream objective, acceptance signal, or owner decision is still ambiguous.
cases.weak_default: Start implementation or produce a long task list from the user's first wording.
evidence_inputs: request contains downstream implementation wording
no explicit L0-L2 goal, contract, or acceptance evidence
no validation signal that would prove the work solved the right problem
expected_judgment: state the upstream problem-definition or contract gap
draft or request the smallest missing decision
park downstream work until the responsible boundary is clear
required_output: validation signal or missing validation evidence
pass_condition: the agent stops or reframes downstream execution because an upstream contract, owner decision, or validation signal is missing
the next action moves to the responsible boundary instead of producing a larger downstream plan
completion_boundary: passes for the tested framing judgment only
does not prove the eventual project decision is correct
forbidden: inventing the business goal
shipping downstream implementation while the upstream contract is ambiguous

cases.scenario: A project is claimed complete before merge, but the evidence contains design intent and partial checks rather than implementation, integration, and acceptance proof.
cases.weak_default: Treat green checks, generated artifacts, or a design document as enough proof for completion.
evidence_inputs: design document describes intended behavior
some checks pass
no explicit owner or user acceptance state is recorded
no direct implementation or acceptance evidence for the claimed user-facing behavior
expected_judgment: separate implementation, validation, integration, and acceptance evidence
make acceptance_state explicit instead of inferring it from validation
downgrade unsupported completion claims to unverified_claims
make the narrowest evidence-supported merge or no-merge call
pass_condition: the completion or merge call changes when implementation, integration, acceptance, or owner evidence is missing
unsupported completion claims are downgraded to missing_evidence or unverified_claims
completion_boundary: passes for evidence weighting on the tested claim only
does not prove the implementation is wrong or right without direct project evidence
forbidden: calling work complete from design intent alone
using passing checks as human or product acceptance

cases.scenario: A memory note or handoff says a project is ready, but current files or command output contradict that state.
cases.weak_default: Repeat the stale summary because it is coherent and easier to use than rereading source.
evidence_inputs: memory or handoff claim says the work is ready
current source, diff, test output, or artifact contradicts the claim
the default output shape would hide the conflict
expected_judgment: let live source override memory or handoff
state the conflict explicitly
override the default output shape if needed to show the evidence boundary
pass_condition: live source or command evidence overrides memory, handoff, or stale local context
the contradiction changes the status, next action, or confidence level
completion_boundary: passes for current-truth priority on the tested claim only
does not prove the stale source should be deleted without a separate retention decision
forbidden: treating stale context as current truth
smoothing over the contradiction to preserve a tidy status report

cases.scenario: A source-work candidate looks aligned with the goal, but it may add process volume instead of raising judgment quality.
cases.weak_default: Accept the candidate because it uses the right vocabulary and passes verification.
evidence_inputs: candidate claims to improve source quality
diff mostly adds rules, templates, or required outputs
no evidence that the change improves reframing, evidence weighting, or boundary judgment
expected_judgment: state the strongest counterargument against the candidate
identify the cheapest evidence that would prove or falsify the improvement
park or narrow the change if it only adds process volume
pass_condition: the strongest counterargument changes the proposed source change, narrows it, or parks it
the replay names the cheapest evidence that would confirm or falsify the source-evolution claim
completion_boundary: passes for source-change critique on the tested claim only
does not prove the whole source iteration is complete
forbidden: equating more SOP text with higher intelligence ceiling
treating verifier success as proof of product value

cases.scenario: The default first-read or minimum-output scaffold is too narrow for the evidence boundary in front of the agent.
cases.weak_default: Follow the default read order and output shape even though it misses the source of truth.
evidence_inputs: default scaffold points to normal project state files
actual claim depends on a different source of truth, artifact, command output, or owner decision
following the default would produce a confident but under-evidenced answer
expected_judgment: name the default being overridden
state the evidence-boundary reason for the override
preserve evidence, confidence, unverified_claims, and concrete next action
pass_condition: the replay names the default scaffold being overridden
the override is justified by evidence boundary and still preserves evidence, confidence, unverified claims, and next action
completion_boundary: passes for the tested scaffold override only
does not authorize skipping required source for convenience
forbidden: skipping required source for convenience
hiding the override as if the default was followed

cases.scenario: A trigger phrase, template field, or verifier string is useful for today's carrier but starts defining the product core or blocking valid source evolution.
cases.weak_default: Keep the scaffold because it prevents some current failures and is already encoded in the verifier.
evidence_inputs: scaffold protects a current workflow shape
same durable boundary can be protected by a semantic or structural check
current exact wording or field shape increases future refactor cost
expected_judgment: separate the durable principle from the current carrier shape
soften, move, or prune the scaffold while preserving boundary protection
record the half-life reason for the change
pass_condition: the replay separates the durable principle from the carrier shape
the resulting action softens, moves, prunes, or protects the scaffold without deleting the core boundary
completion_boundary: passes for half-life separation on the tested scaffold only
does not prove all source scaffolding has been reviewed
forbidden: deleting a core boundary because one scaffold is stale
freezing incidental prose shape as a product contract

cases.scenario: A strategy, research, product, architecture, or organization question is still open enough that a single favored recommendation would hide material alternatives.
cases.weak_default: Recommend one polished path before the problem definition, comparison criteria, and competing paths are explicit.
evidence_inputs: request asks for strategy, research, product, architecture, or cross-stakeholder judgment
problem definition or success criteria are present enough to compare options
multiple plausible solution levels, time horizons, or risk appetites could satisfy the objective
expected_judgment: generate multiple comparable solution paths after the Define gate
state comparison criteria and evidence gaps for each path
recommend a path only after explaining why the alternatives are weaker or deferred
pass_condition: the replay shows at least two plausible paths before final recommendation
the selected path is justified by criteria, evidence strength, constraints, or pilotability rather than preference
completion_boundary: passes for alternative-path generation and comparison on the tested decision only
does not prove the final recommendation succeeds without applied validation
forbidden: one-solution recommendation before Define is explicit
listing alternatives without comparing evidence, constraints, or validation path

cases.scenario: A loop, routine, recurring check, or automation proposal sounds useful, but the goal, authority, independent sensor, feedback path, or cost boundary is missing.
cases.weak_default: Run or schedule the loop because repeated agent activity looks like progress.
evidence_inputs: proposal asks the agent to keep checking, iterate, or automate
one or more of goal, authority, independent sensor, feedback path, or cost/stop boundary is missing
no evidence shows repeated execution would change the next action
expected_judgment: require a clear goal, scoped authority, independent sensor, feedback-to-next-action path, and cost or stop boundary
keep the loop report-first or park automation until missing evidence is supplied
preserve human approval for scheduling, external posting, risky writes, and source-of-truth changes
pass_condition: the replay parks automation or keeps the loop report-first when required gate evidence is missing
the final call changes when the independent sensor cannot change the next action
completion_boundary: passes for loop verification and authority gating on the tested proposal only
does not prove a future scheduled routine is worthwhile without manual value evidence
forbidden: treating repeated agent activity as improvement
scheduling or running automation without an independent sensor
upgrading authority without explicit human approval

cases.scenario: A decomposed insight candidate looks reusable, but it aligns to no intelligence-ceiling or half-life signal and is being pushed toward a tracked patch.
cases.weak_default: Grade the candidate as worth landing and open a patch-candidate because it sounds like a good idea.
evidence_inputs: candidate is a clean, transferable mechanism
no intelligence_ceiling_signals or half_life_signals entry actually applies
the round is under pressure to produce a tracked source change
expected_judgment: name whether any vision signal aligns before grading source_value
assign source_value from the aligned signal, not from surface appeal
park or mark no-change when no vision signal aligns instead of opening a patch-candidate
pass_condition: the call changes to park or no-change when no vision signal aligns
source_value is justified by a named signal rather than preference
completion_boundary: passes for vision-value alignment on the tested candidate only
does not prove an aligned keep actually raises the ceiling or half-life without an applied run
forbidden: opening a patch-candidate with no aligned vision signal
treating surface novelty as vision value

cases.scenario: A decomposed insight candidate does align to an intelligence-ceiling or half-life signal and is being proposed as a patch-candidate.
cases.weak_default: Accept the aligned candidate as a patch-candidate without grading source_value, naming the changed judgment call, or choosing where and how small it lands.
evidence_inputs: candidate aligns to a named intelligence_ceiling_signals or half_life_signals entry
the round can point to which judgment call the candidate would change
a target source layer and a smallest source surface are available to carry the rule
expected_judgment: grade source_value high|medium|low from what judgment call the candidate changes, not from surface appeal
name the changed judgment call and choose the landing_layer and smallest_source_surface
require replay support and the owner gate before the patch-candidate becomes a tracked patch
pass_condition: source_value is justified by a named changed judgment call rather than novelty
the keep names both a landing_layer and a smallest_source_surface and still defers the tracked patch to replay and owner gate
completion_boundary: passes for vision-value grading and landing judgment on the tested candidate only
does not prove the aligned keep actually raises the ceiling or half-life without an applied run
forbidden: grading source_value from reusability or novelty instead of a changed judgment call
advancing to a tracked patch without a landing_layer, replay support, or owner gate

cases.scenario: A user asks the agent to start project execution, but the current delivery state, target artifact or decision, next action, human or agent gate, validation evidence, or feedback route is missing.
cases.weak_default: Start implementation because the requested task sounds executable and the agent can produce code or documents.
evidence_inputs: request asks for project work or implementation
one or more of delivery state, target artifact or decision, next action, gate, validation evidence, or feedback route is missing
missing delivery-loop evidence could hide an upstream human decision, false completion risk, or untestable output
expected_judgment: state the current delivery state before execution
name the target artifact or decision, next action, and the human or agent gate
make an explicit advance, hold, or rework call from gate status and validation evidence instead of advancing from a state label alone
name the validation method, validation evidence or missing evidence, and feedback route to the next action
route comments, findings, failures, or acceptance gaps to responsible boundary, rework items, changed artifacts, validation method, and open residue
only propose durable memory for reusable boundary judgments, verified project facts, or action-changing evidence
pause, reframe, or narrow execution when any required delivery-loop field is missing
pass_condition: execution is reframed, paused, or narrowed when state, artifact or decision, next action, gate, validation evidence, or feedback route is missing
the next action changes when a human-owned gate or missing validation evidence is identified

delivery advances only when the target artifact exists or target decision is resolved, every applicable human-owned gate is cleared, and validation evidence supports the next action; otherwise it holds or reworks
feedback from comments, findings, failures, or acceptance gaps routes to a responsible boundary, rework item, changed artifact, validation method, and open residue
durable memory candidates are limited to reusable boundary judgments, verified project facts, or action-changing evidence
completion_boundary: passes for project delivery-loop judgment on the tested task only
does not prove the eventual implementation is correct without project validation and acceptance evidence
forbidden: task-only implementation without delivery state
hidden human gate
advancing from a delivery-state label without gate status and validation evidence
feedback without responsible boundary, rework item, changed artifact, validation method, and open residue
durable memory update from unverified or one-off project noise
